CodeAnt AI is reviewing your PR.

## Reviewer's Guide Adds Photon-specific SPSC ring buffer telemetry counters and wires them into dispatch/consumer code paths, exposing their values in the metrics summary while preserving lock-free semantics. #### Sequence diagram for Photon SPSC dispatch and fleet telemetry path ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant SIMA_Dispatch participant PhotonDispatchRing participant SIMA_Fleet rect rgb(230,230,230) SIMA_Dispatch->>SIMA_Dispatch: TrackPhotonPoolExhausted() note over SIMA_Dispatch: Pool exhausted fallback to heap alloc end alt pool_slot_index_valid SIMA_Dispatch->>PhotonDispatchRing: TryEnqueue(ref slot) alt enqueue_success SIMA_Dispatch->>SIMA_Dispatch: TrackPhotonEnqueue() else ring_full SIMA_Dispatch->>SIMA_Dispatch: TrackPhotonRingFull() note over SIMA_Dispatch: Fallback to ConcurrentQueue end end PhotonDispatchRing-->>SIMA_Fleet: TryDequeue(out ringSlot) alt dequeue_success SIMA_Fleet->>SIMA_Fleet: TrackPhotonDequeue() SIMA_Fleet->>SIMA_Fleet: VerifyPhotonSlotIntegrity(ref ringSlot, sideband) alt crc_mismatch SIMA_Fleet->>SIMA_Fleet: TrackPhotonCrcFailure() end end ``` #### Flow diagram for Photon telemetry counters lifecycle ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Dispatch/Fleet runtime] --> B[TrackPhotonEnqueue] A --> C[TrackPhotonDequeue] A --> D[TrackPhotonRingFull] A --> E[TrackPhotonPoolExhausted] A --> F[TrackPhotonCrcFailure] subgraph Counters B --> G[_metricPhotonEnqueues] C --> H[_metricPhotonDequeues] D --> I[_metricPhotonRingFull] E --> J[_metricPhotonPoolExhausted] F --> K[_metricPhotonCrcFailures] end L[ResetTelemetry] --> G L --> H L --> I L --> J L --> K M[EmitMetricsSummary] --> N[Interlocked.Read photon counters] N --> O[Print Photon metrics in summary] ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Introduce Photon SPSC ring buffer telemetry counters and integrate them with reset and metrics summary. |
  • Declare Photon telemetry counters for enqueues, dequeues, ring full, pool exhausted, and CRC failures as long fields.
  • Reset all Photon counters in the existing ResetTelemetry method using Interlocked.Exchange.
  • Expose Photon counters in EmitMetricsSummary by reading them via Interlocked.Read and printing them alongside existing metrics.
| `src/V12_002.Telemetry.cs` | | Wire Photon telemetry tracking into dispatch producer code paths. |
  • Track pool exhaustion when Photon pool allocation fails and heap fallback is used.
  • Track successful ring buffer enqueues on producer hot paths for both market and limit-order slots.
  • Track ring-full events when producer falls back from the ring buffer to ConcurrentQueue.
| `src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs` | | Wire Photon telemetry tracking into fleet consumer and integrity verification paths. |
  • Track successful Photon ring buffer dequeues in the fleet pump loop.
  • Track CRC / XorShadow integrity failures in VerifyPhotonSlotIntegrity by incrementing the Photon CRC failure counter and emitting a diagnostic Print.
| `src/V12_002.SIMA.Fleet.cs` | ---
Tips and commands #### Interacting with Sourcery - **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request. - **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments. - **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it. - **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time. - **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to (re-)generate the summary at any time. - **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time. - **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore. - **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review! #### Customizing Your Experience Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to: - Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request summary, the reviewer's guide, and others. - Change the review language. - Add, remove or edit custom review instructions. - Adjust other review settings. #### Getting Help - [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback. - Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information. - Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/pull/7?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack) ## Walkthrough The pull request adds instrumentation to track Photon ring-buffer metrics across the dispatch and fleet pipelines. Five lock-free counters are defined to capture enqueues, dequeues, ring-full fallbacks, pool exhaustion events, and CRC integrity failures. These counters are reset during telemetry reset and incremented by helper methods at event sites, then reported in session metrics output. ## Changes **Photon Ring-Buffer Telemetry Instrumentation** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Telemetry counters and helpers**
`src/V12_002.Telemetry.cs` | Five new private lock-free counters track Photon enqueues, dequeues, ring-full fallbacks, pool-exhaustion fallbacks, and CRC failures. `ResetTelemetry()` zeros them, and five new `TrackPhoton*()` helper methods increment them via `Interlocked.Increment()`. | | **Dispatch path telemetry**
`src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs` | Enqueue/publish paths record `TrackPhotonPoolExhausted()` when the pool is exhausted, `TrackPhotonEnqueue()` after successful market and limit dispatch enqueues, and `TrackPhotonRingFull()` when the ring is full and control falls back to the legacy queue. | | **Fleet dequeue and integrity telemetry**
`src/V12_002.SIMA.Fleet.cs` | The dequeue path records `TrackPhotonDequeue()` after successfully retrieving a ring slot, and the integrity check records `TrackPhotonCrcFailure()` when shadow value verification fails. | | **Metrics aggregation and output**
`src/V12_002.Telemetry.cs` | `EmitMetricsSummary()` reads the new Photon counters via `Interlocked.Read()`, and session output appends five new lines reporting enqueues/dequeues, ring-full events, pool exhaustion, and CRC failures. | --- ## Estimated code review effort ­ƒÄ» 2 (Simple) | ÔÅ▒´©Å ~12 minutes

The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. | | Description check | ÔÜá´©Å Warning | PR description is extensive but fails to complete required template sections. Build Tag and explicit Mission field remain as unfilled placeholders. Files Changed section shows only generic 'src/...' without specific file rationales. Pre-Flight Checklist items are unchecked boxes with no confirmation of actual test passage. | Fill in Build Tag (e.g., BUILD_1105, GAP-2), add a single-sentence Mission description, list each changed file with its specific rationale, and check/confirm actual passage of all mandatory gates (ASCII, lock-free audit, lint, build, AMAL, Bob Shell, deploy-sync, BUILD_TAG banner verification). |
Ô£à Passed checks (3 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Title check | Ô£à Passed | The title accurately and specifically describes the main change: integrating telemetry for the SPSC (single-producer single-consumer) Photon ring buffer in the lock-free order dispatch path. | | Linked Issues check | Ô£à Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | Ô£à Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
Ô£Å´©Å Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.

Reviewing your code
Code Review ÔÜá´©Å Changes requested 0 resolved / 1 findings Integrates SPSC ring buffer telemetry across dispatch and fleet paths, but missing instrumentation in limit-order fallback paths needs correction before merging.
ÔÜá´©Å Bug: Missing telemetry in limit-order dispatch fallback paths ­ƒôä src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs:938-941 ­ƒôä src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs:999-1007 The limit-order dispatch path (around lines 938-941 and 999-1007) is missing `TrackPhotonPoolExhausted()` and `TrackPhotonRingFull()` calls that are present in the equivalent market-order path (lines 770 and 844). This means Photon metrics will under-report pool exhaustion and ring-full events whenever limit orders hit these fallback conditions, giving an inaccurate picture of ring buffer health.
Add TrackPhotonPoolExhausted() in the limit-order pool-exhausted branch and TrackPhotonRingFull() in the limit-order ring-full branch, matching the market-order path. ```` // In the pool-exhausted else branch (~line 939): else { TrackPhotonPoolExhausted(); _proxyOrdersLmt = new Order[MaxOrdersPerSlot]; _poolSlotIndexLmt = -1; } // In the ring-full else branch (~line 1001): if (_poolSlotIndexLmt >= 0) { TrackPhotonRingFull(); Order[] legacyOrdersLmt = new Order[] { entry }; _photonPool.ReleaseByIndex(_poolSlotIndexLmt); _photonSideband[_poolSlotIndexLmt] = default(FleetDispatchSideband); _proxyOrdersLmt = legacyOrdersLmt; } ````
­ƒñû Prompt for agents ```` Code Review: Integrates SPSC ring buffer telemetry across dispatch and fleet paths, but missing instrumentation in limit-order fallback paths needs correction before merging. 1. ÔÜá´©Å Bug: Missing telemetry in limit-order dispatch fallback paths Files: src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs:938-941, src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs:999-1007 The limit-order dispatch path (around lines 938-941 and 999-1007) is missing `TrackPhotonPoolExhausted()` and `TrackPhotonRingFull()` calls that are present in the equivalent market-order path (lines 770 and 844). This means Photon metrics will under-report pool exhaustion and ring-full events whenever limit orders hit these fallback conditions, giving an inaccurate picture of ring buffer health. Fix (Add TrackPhotonPoolExhausted() in the limit-order pool-exhausted branch and TrackPhotonRingFull() in the limit-order ring-full branch, matching the market-order path.): // In the pool-exhausted else branch (~line 939): else { TrackPhotonPoolExhausted(); _proxyOrdersLmt = new Order[MaxOrdersPerSlot]; _poolSlotIndexLmt = -1; } // In the ring-full else branch (~line 1001): if (_poolSlotIndexLmt >= 0) { TrackPhotonRingFull(); Order[] legacyOrdersLmt = new Order[] { entry }; _photonPool.ReleaseByIndex(_poolSlotIndexLmt); _photonSideband[_poolSlotIndexLmt] = default(FleetDispatchSideband); _proxyOrdersLmt = legacyOrdersLmt; } ````

reviews.body:
The PR successfully integrates Photon SPSC ring buffer telemetry tracking into the lock-free dispatch system. The implementation is clean and follows the established patterns:

**Implementation Quality:**
- All tracking calls properly placed at critical path events (enqueue, dequeue, fallbacks, integrity failures)
- Lock-free telemetry using Interlocked operations maintains zero-lock semantics
- Comprehensive coverage in EmitMetricsSummary for end-of-session diagnostics
- Field initialization, reset logic, and reporting are consistent

**Architecture Alignment:**
- Maintains lock-free guarantees (Interlocked-only, no lock() statements)
- Zero-allocation hot path (tracking calls add no heap pressure)
- Follows existing telemetry patterns established for FSM/SIMA/Reaper metrics

The changes are purely additive with no modifications to existing logic. All telemetry infrastructure is correctly wired from declaration through reset to reporting.

---
You can now have the agent implement changes and create commits directly on your pull request's source branch. Simply comment with /q followed by your request in natural language to ask the agent to make changes.

Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `VerifyPhotonSlotIntegrity` you both call `TrackPhotonCrcFailure()` and manually `Interlocked.Increment(ref _photonCrcFailures)`, which will either double-count or fail to compile now that `_metricPhotonCrcFailures` is the backing fieldÔÇöstandardize on the `TrackPhotonCrcFailure` helper and remove the direct increment (or update the field reference) to keep the telemetry consistent.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `VerifyPhotonSlotIntegrity` you both call `TrackPhotonCrcFailure()` and manually `Interlocked.Increment(ref _photonCrcFailures)`, which will either double-count or fail to compile now that `_metricPhotonCrcFailures` is the backing fieldÔÇöstandardize on the `TrackPhotonCrcFailure` helper and remove the direct increment (or update the field reference) to keep the telemetry consistent.
~~~

</details>

**2 issues found** across 3 files
Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid ÔÇö if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P2: Missing `TrackPhotonDequeue()` call in the `DrainAllDispatchQueuesOnAbort()` loop. The abort drain path successfully dequeues from the photon ring but doesn't increment the dequeue telemetry counter, causing `_metricPhotonDequeues` to undercount relative to `_metricPhotonEnqueues`. P2: Telemetry is incomplete in `Dispatch_PublishLimitEntryToPhoton`: `TrackPhotonPoolExhausted()` and `TrackPhotonRingFull()` are missing from this path, while the parallel `Dispatch_PublishMarketBracketToPhoton` method has all three calls. Limit-entry pool-exhaustion and ring-full events will go untracked in session metrics. ```
Reply with feedback, questions, or to request a fix.
